source_title: Synaptic and dendritic architecture of different types of hippocampal somatostatin interneurons
node_relevance_reason: Directly characterises OLM and hippocampo-septal (HS) cells in CA1 with detailed anatomical, electrophysiological and synaptic input comparison; highly relevant to P1 (soma location), P5 (HS cell) and bistratified cell nodes
summary: Two major SOM-containing interneuron types in CA1 are OLM and hippocampo-septal (HS) cells; both share soma-dendritic architecture but differ in axonal targets and in vivo activity. Quantitative 3D electron microscopy reconstructions show OLM cells receive ~8400 synaptic inputs while HS cells receive ~15600, with ~16% GABAergic in both. Data include patch-clamp electrophysiology and compartmental modelling enabling precise comparison of intrinsic properties.
quotes: Two main types of SOM-containing cells in area CA1 of the hippocampus are oriens-lacunosum-moleculare (OLM) cells and hippocampo-septal (HS) cells. These cell types show many similarities in their soma-dendritic architecture, but they have different axonal targets, display different activity patterns in vivo and are thought to have distinct network functions.
we estimated that an OLM cell receives about 8400, whereas an HS cell about 15600 synaptic inputs, about 16% of which are GABAergic

source_title: Dendritic Inhibition Provided by Interneuron-Specific Cells Controls the Firing Rate and Timing of the Hippocampal Feedback Inhibitory Circuitry
section: Identification and functional connectivity of IS3 cells
node_relevance_reason: Direct characterisation of IS3 cells including soma location, axonal targets, marker co-expression (VIP+CR), morphological subtypes, and functional connectivity to OLM cells; directly addresses P3 (IS subtype split) and IS interneuron node
summary: IS3 cells in mouse hippocampus co-express VIP and CR and have somas in stratum pyramidale or radiatum, sending axons to the oriens/alveus to contact OLM cells preferentially. The study confirms that IS3 cells preferentially innervate the O/A border (with occasional RAD and PYR branches) and have unipolar (47%) dendritic arbors consistent with preferential recruitment through Schaffer collateral input. Multiple criteria are required to discriminate IS cells from other GABAergic interneurons.
quotes: IS3 cells in the mouse hippocampus were similar to those described originally in the rat hippocampus. These cells innervated heavily the O/A border, although occasional axonal branches were found in the RAD and PYR. Their dendritic arbors were often unipolar (47% of cells), suggesting that these cells are preferentially recruited through the activation of the Schaffer collaterals
although IS3 cells can be distinguished based on the coexpression of VIP and CR, VIP and CR alone are expressed by several distinct types of hippocampal interneurons

source_title: Dendritic Inhibition Provided by Interneuron-Specific Cells Controls the Firing Rate and Timing of the Hippocampal Feedback Inhibitory Circuitry
node_relevance_reason: Abstract confirms IS3 cells express VIP+CR, soma in PYR/RAD, axon to O/A, target OLM cells preferentially; VIP basket cells do NOT connect with O/A interneurons; relevant to IS interneuron node and P3
summary: IS3 cells (VIP+/CR+) in CA1 O/A preferentially innervate OLM cells through dendritic synapses; VIP-positive basket cells provide perisomatic inhibition to pyramidal neurons with asynchronous GABA release and are NOT connected to O/A interneurons. IS3-OLM synapses have small amplitude and low release probability but summated efficiently at high frequency, and synchronous IS3 activity controls OLM firing rate and timing.
quotes: type 3 IS (IS3) cells that coexpress the vasoactive intestinal polypeptide (VIP) and calretinin contact several distinct types of interneurons within the hippocampal CA1 stratum oriens/alveus (O/A), with preferential innervation of oriens-lacunosum moleculare cells (OLMs) through dendritic synapses
VIP-positive basket cells provided perisomatic inhibition to CA1 pyramidal neurons with the asynchronous GABA release and were not connected with O/A interneurons

source_title: Dendritic Inhibition Provided by Interneuron-Specific Cells Controls the Firing Rate and Timing of the Hippocampal Feedback Inhibitory Circuitry
node_relevance_reason: Distinguishes IS1 from IS3 subtypes by anatomy: IS1 has cell body in O/A, PYR, or RAD with wide-projecting axon in RAD/PYR and dendro-dendritically connected clusters; IS3 has soma in RAD/PYR with axon projecting to O/A; directly supports P3 IS subtype split
summary: Using CR-Cre mice, at least two subtypes of CR-positive interneurons were identified in CA1 RAD/PYR. The first (IS1) has soma in O/A, PYR, or RAD, multipolar dendrites, axon mainly in RAD/PYR, dendro-dendritically connected clusters, and high input resistance (~483 MΩ). The second (IS3) has soma in RAD/PYR, unipolar/bipolar dendrites, and axon projecting to O/A. The two subtypes could only be distinguished anatomically.
quotes: One group of CR-positive cells had a cell body located within the O/A, PYR, or RAD; a multipolar dendritic tree; and a widely projecting axon that mostly occupied the CA1 RAD and PYR (ϳ65%; Fig. 6C1). These cells had a high input resistance (482.5 Ϯ 98.2 M⍀; n ϭ 4)

In several cases (n ϭ 5 slices/2 animals), the dendrites of 2-3 CR-positive cells of this subtype were found to form dendro-dendritically connected clusters (Fig. 6C1, top inset), which is a hallmark of the type I interneuron-specific (IS1) interneuron described previously in the rat CA1 hippocampus

source_title: Dendritic Inhibition Provided by Interneuron-Specific Cells Controls the Firing Rate and Timing of the Hippocampal Feedback Inhibitory Circuitry
section: Identification and functional connectivity of IS3 cells
node_relevance_reason: Describes IS1 cells as CR+, heterogeneous in firing properties, with soma in RAD/PYR and wide RAD/PYR axon; notes IS1 group may contain more than one subtype; supports P3
summary: Using the CR-Cre mouse model, IS1-type CR+ interneurons were characterised in RAD/PYR: they showed remarkable anatomical diversity, with cell bodies in different layers and axons mostly in RAD/PYR, high input resistance, and heterogeneous firing properties. The study concludes that more than one subtype of putative IS1 cells likely exists within this CR+ group.
quotes: This subtype of IS cells exhibited a remarkable anatomical diversity, with cell bodies that occupied different layers and an axon that projected mostly within the RAD and PYR. Similar to IS3 cells, IS1 cells exhibited a high input resistance but were more heterogeneous in terms of firing properties. Therefore, more than one subtype of putative IS1 cells is likely to be found within this group of CR-positive interneurons.

source_title: Dendritic Inhibition Provided by Interneuron-Specific Cells Controls the Firing Rate and Timing of the Hippocampal Feedback Inhibitory Circuitry
node_relevance_reason: Introduces IS cell classification: IS1 (CR+), IS3 (VIP+/CR+, soma in PYR/RAD, axon to O/A), targets OLM cells; VIP expression by IS cells noted; relevant to IS interneuron node P3
summary: IS cells were subdivided into three subtypes with distinct anatomical and neurochemical features. IS3 somas are in stratum pyramidale or radiatum, axons go to O/A to contact interneurons, preferentially mGluR1a+ OLM cells. IS3 cells target OLMs, bistratified cells, and oriens-oriens interneurons, and synchronous IS3 activity controls postsynaptic cell firing rate and timing.
quotes: The somas of these cells are located in the stratum pyramidale (PYR) or radiatum (RAD) and send their axons to the oriens-alveus (O/A) to contact other interneurons located in this area, preferentially metabotropic glutamate receptor 1a (mGluR1a)-positive oriens-lacunosum moleculare interneurons (OLMs)

source_title: Functional specialization of hippocampal somatostatin-expressing interneurons
node_relevance_reason: Directly bridges transcriptomic Sst-IN subtypes to morpho-functional identities using combinatorial Cre/Flp intersectional approach; identifies Sst;;Tac1 intersection as bistratified cells and Ndnf;;Nkx2-1 as OLM cells; addresses P2 (bridging datasets), P4 (Tac1 for bistratified, PV+ OLM subpopulation, Ndnf protein)
summary: Using four mouse intersectional models (Sst;;Tac1, Ndnf;;Nkx2-1, and others) in CA1, functionally distinct Sst-IN subpopulations were identified that tile the morpho-functional parameter space of the Sst superfamily. The Sst;;Tac1 intersection revealed bistratified INs that preferentially synapse onto fast-spiking interneurons (FS-INs) and can interrupt their firing. The Ndnf;;Nkx2-1 intersection identified OLM INs that predominantly target CA1 pyramidal neurons and avoid FS-INs.
quotes: the Sst;;Tac1 intersection revealed a population of bistratified INs that preferentially synapsed onto fast-spiking interneurons (FS-INs) and were both necessary and sufficient to interrupt their firing
the Ndnf;;Nkx2-1 intersection identified a population of oriens lacunosum-moleculare (OLM) INs that predominantly targeted CA1 pyramidal neurons, avoiding FS-INs

source_title: Common Origins of Hippocampal Ivy and Nitric Oxide Synthase Expressing Neurogliaform Cells
node_relevance_reason: Provides key evidence that IvC and nNOS+ NGC share overlapping developmental, electrophysiological, morphological, and neurochemical properties; nNOS+ NGCs are MGE-derived while nNOS- NGCs are CGE-derived; directly addresses P3 (IvC vs NGC boundary) and P4 (Nos1 universality in NGC)
summary: Ivy cells (IvCs) and nNOS+ neurogliaform cells (NGCs) have completely overlapping developmental, electrophysiological, morphological, and neurochemical properties, suggesting they constitute a single unique interneuron subtype despite differences in laminar organisation. nNOS+ NGCs are MGE-derived (under Nkx2-1 control), while nNOS− NGCs are CGE-derived. The laminar positions of IvCs and NGCs may reflect different functional roles despite shared properties.
quotes: our findings reveal that IvCs and nNOS ϩ NGCs have completely overlapping developmental, electrophysiological, morphological, and neurochemical properties, suggesting that these cells constitute a single unique interneuron subtype despite differences in laminar organization
classically defined NGCs can be subdivided into two groups based on nNOS expression and lineage with nNOS ϩ NGCs being derived from the MGE and nNOS Ϫ NGCs arising from CGE progenitors

source_title: Common Origins of Hippocampal Ivy and Nitric Oxide Synthase Expressing Neurogliaform Cells
node_relevance_reason: Abstract confirms NGCs and IvCs share neurochemical and electrophysiological signatures (NPY+, late spiking), both derive from MGE under Nkx2-1, but a nNOS- NGC subset arises from CGE; directly addresses P3 and P4
summary: NGCs and IvCs represent the main nNOS+ hippocampal interneurons; they share NPY expression and late-spiking firing patterns. Lineage analyses show that nNOS+ IvCs and NGCs both derive from MGE progenitors under Nkx2-1 control, while a distinct nNOS- NGC subpopulation arises from CGE progenitors.
quotes: nNOS+ IvCs and NGCs are both derived from medial ganglionic eminence (MGE) progenitors under control of the transcription factor Nkx2-1

a subset of NGCs lacking nNOS arises from caudal ganglionic eminence (CGE) progenitors. Thus, while nNOS+ NGCs and IvCs arise from MGE progenitors, a CGE origin distinguishes a discrete population of nNOS− NGCs

source_title: Common Origins of Hippocampal Ivy and Nitric Oxide Synthase Expressing Neurogliaform Cells
node_relevance_reason: Addresses nNOS expression across NGC subtypes: nNOS+ NGCs are the majority in hippocampus (MGE-derived), nNOS- NGCs are a minor CGE population; contrasts with neocortex where CGE-derived NGCs dominate; directly addresses P4 Nos1 universality
summary: In hippocampus, the nNOS+ (MGE-derived) subset represents the majority of all NGCs, with only minor contribution from CGE-derived (nNOS-) NGCs — the reverse of the neocortex. NPY expression is activity-dependent, which may explain similar NPY levels across both NGC groups despite different origins. The study cannot rule out that IvCs and nNOS+ NGCs perform distinct functions due to their different laminar positions.
quotes: we found that the nNOS ϩ subset, hence MGE derived, represents the majority of all hippocampal NGCs with a minor contribution from CGE-derived NGCs. This contrasts with findings in the neocortex in which fate-mapping evidence supports a dominant CGE origin for NGCs

source_title: Common Origins of Hippocampal Ivy and Nitric Oxide Synthase Expressing Neurogliaform Cells
node_relevance_reason: Historically NGCs were considered NPY+/nNOS+ homogeneous; this study's NPY-GFP characterisation reveals nNOS+ majority vs nNOS- minor CGE subset; supports P3 boundary between IvC and NGC based on nNOS
summary: Historically NGCs were viewed as functionally homogeneous with NPY and frequent nNOS co-expression. In the hippocampus, the nNOS+ (MGE-derived) NGC subset is the majority. CGE-derived nNOS- NGCs are a minor population possibly explaining lower nNOS levels seen in neocortex. Regional difference in NGC origins between hippocampus and neocortex is noted.
quotes: Historically, NGCs have been viewed as a functionally homogenous interneuron subtype characterized by the expression of NPY with frequent coexpression of nNOS

source_title: Transcriptional and morphological profiling of parvalbumin interneuron subpopulations in the mouse hippocampus
node_relevance_reason: Patch-seq study of PV+ interneurons in mouse CA1 showing transcriptomic continuity across morphologically distinct subtypes; directly addresses P2 (bridging datasets) and P4 (PV+ subpopulation transcriptomic boundaries including axo-axonic vs basket)
summary: Patch-seq applied to morphologically identified PV+ interneurons in mouse CA1 showed high transcriptomic similarity across subtypes (basket, axo-axonic, bistratified), with few genes showing divergent expression between morphological types. PV-INs show a uniform synaptic cell adhesion molecule (CAM) profile, suggesting CAM expression in mature PV cells does not reflect wiring specificity after development. Results indicate transcriptomic and biophysical landscapes are continuous and not predictive of distinct morphological identities.
quotes: we find high transcriptomic similarity among PV-INs, with few genes showing divergent expression between morphologically different types
PV-INs show a uniform synaptic cell adhesion molecule (CAM) profile, suggesting that CAM expression in mature PV cells does not reflect wiring specificity after development
while PV-INs differ in anatomy and in vivo activity, their continuous transcriptomic and homogenous biophysical landscapes are not predictive of these distinct identities

source_title: Discrete interneuron subsets participate in GluN1/GluN3A excitatory glycine receptor (eGlyR)-mediated regulation of hippocampal network activity throughout development and evolution
node_relevance_reason: Uses multi-institute integrated scRNA-seq dataset to confirm Sst-IN transcriptomic identity and discovers Grin3a expression in NGFCs; classifies IvCs as Lamp5+/Lhx6+ MGE-derived and CGE-derived NGFCs as Lamp5+/Lhx6-; highly relevant to P2 (transcriptomic bridging) and P3 (IvC vs NGC boundary)
summary: Multi-institute integrated scRNA-seq of 86,852 GABAergic interneuron transcriptomes confirmed that most Sst-INs express high Grin3a and discovered Grin3a expression across most NGFC subsets. Ivy cells (IvCs) are classified as Lamp5+/Lhx6+ (MGE-derived), while CGE-derived NGFCs are Lamp5+/Lhx6-/Ndnf+. In vivo, mClover-GluN3A labelled cells co-expressed Sst in stratum oriens and nNOS in putative NGFCs in stratum lacunosum moleculare.
quotes: Amongst NGFCs Grin3a is expressed in both Lamp5 + /Lhx6 + MGE-derived subsets, also known as Ivy cells (IvyCs), and Lamp5 + /Lhx6 -caudal ganglionic eminence (CGE)-derived subsets that are both well represented in the rodent hippocampus

mClover-GluN3A labeled cells co-expressed Sst in stratum oriens and neuronal nitric oxide synthase (nNOS) in putaZve NGFCs that comprise the dominant interneuron subtype in stratum lacunosum moleculare

source_title: Discrete interneuron subsets participate in GluN1/GluN3A excitatory glycine receptor (eGlyR)-mediated regulation of hippocampal network activity throughout development and evolution
section: Multi-institute single-cell transcriptome analyses of GABAergic interneurons
node_relevance_reason: Provides canonical marker gene combinations used for interneuron subtype annotation in the multi-institute scRNA-seq dataset; MGE markers (Lhx6, Pvalb, Sst, Id2) and CGE markers (Sncg, Vip, Calb2, Meis2, Cnr1, Ntng1, Ndnf); NGFC.M = Lhx6+/Lamp5+/Id2+; NGFC.C = Lhx6-/Lamp5+/Id2+/Ndnf+
summary: The multi-institute scRNA-seq dataset (86,852 GABAergic transcriptomes) used established marker gene combinations: MGE-derived interneurons were annotated by Gad1+ combined with Lhx6, Pvalb, Sst, Id2; CGE-derived by Lhx6-, Sncg, Vip, Calb2, Meis2, Cnr1, Ntng1, Ndnf. MGE-derived NGFCs were annotated Lhx6+/Lamp5+/Id2+ (NGFC.M); CGE-derived NGFCs as Lhx6-/Lamp5+/Id2+/Ndnf+ (NGFC.C).
quotes: The Neurogliaform interneurons that are Lhx6 + /Lamp5 + /Id2 + are annotated as MGE-derived (NGFC.M); and those that are Lhx6 - /Lamp5 + /Id2 + /Ndnf + are annotated as CGE-derived (NGFC.C)

source_title: Behavior‐dependent activity patterns of GABAergic long‐range projecting neurons in the rat hippocampus
node_relevance_reason: Provides key context for hippocampo-septal (HS) cell characterisation: distinguishes SOM-expressing long-range projecting cells from locally terminating SOM interneurons (OLM, bistratified); directly relevant to P5 (HS cell) and P1 (soma location in stratum oriens)
summary: In rat CA1, at least five distinct SOM-expressing GABAergic interneuron types have been defined, innervating different proximo-distal dendritic domains of pyramidal cells. In addition to locally terminating SOM interneurons (OLM, bistratified), a diverse population of long-range SOM-expressing GABAergic neurons projects to the medial septum and/or retrohippocampal cortical areas.
quotes: In the rat hippocampal CA1 area, at least five distinct types of SOMexpressing GABAergic neuron have been defined
a diverse population of longrange SOM-expressing GABAergic neurons send projections to the medial septum and/or retrohippocampal cortical areas

source_title: Dendritic Inhibition Provided by Interneuron-Specific Cells Controls the Firing Rate and Timing of the Hippocampal Feedback Inhibitory Circuitry
node_relevance_reason: General background on hippocampal interneuron diversity and cell-type-specific activity; provides context but no specific data on individual interneuron types in the NODE CONTEXT
summary: Background on hippocampal interneuron diversity: different subtypes provide perisomatic vs dendritic inhibition to pyramidal cells, and interneurons show cell-type-specific and activity-dependent recruitment during hippocampal oscillations in different behavioural states.
quotes: Some interneuron subtypes provide perisomatic inhibition to pyramidal cells (PCs), whereas others innervate different dendritic domains of PCs.

source_title: Common Origins of Hippocampal Ivy and Nitric Oxide Synthase Expressing Neurogliaform Cells
node_relevance_reason: Provides lineage context: nNOS interneurons do not overlap with PV or SOM; NNos strongly colocalises with NPY in IvCs and a subset of NGCs; supports nNOS marker discussions for NGC/IvC nodes
summary: In the hippocampus, nNOS-expressing interneurons do not overlap with PV or SOM interneurons and largely segregate from CR interneurons. nNOS strongly co-localises with NPY in both IvCs and a subset of NGCs; based on laminar positioning, IvCs and NGCs are considered distinct subtypes but share electrophysiological, anatomical and molecular properties suggesting they may belong to the same family.
quotes: in the hippocampus, the neuronal isoform of nitric oxide synthase (nNOS) is expressed in an interneuron subpopulation that does not overlap with PV or SOM interneurons and also largely segregates from CR interneurons
nNOS strongly colocalizes with neuropeptide Y in both Ivy cells (IvCs) and a subset of neurogliaform cells (NGCs)

source_title: Discrete interneuron subsets participate in GluN1/GluN3A excitatory glycine receptor (eGlyR)-mediated regulation of hippocampal network activity throughout development and evolution
node_relevance_reason: Introduces Sst-IN eGlyR expression context and references multi-institute dataset; relevant to Sst-expressing interneurons broadly (OLM, bistratified, HS) but no specific subtype detail
summary: MGE-derived hippocampal interneurons express high Grin3a throughout development; adult Sst-INs exhibit robust eGlyR-mediated currents. The study uses transcriptional profiling, conditional loss-of-function, IHC, ex vivo electrophysiology and optogenetics to show Sst-INs and NGFCs express functional eGlyRs from early postnatal through adult.
quotes: adult somatostaZn-expressing interneurons (Sst-INs) exhibit robust eGlyRmediated currents

source_title: Behavior‐dependent activity patterns of GABAergic long‐range projecting neurons in the rat hippocampus
node_relevance_reason: Notes multiple SOM-expressing interneuron types in stratum oriens (OLM, bistratified, basket, axo-axonic, long-range projecting); provides in vivo recording context for SOM+ cells, relevant to multiple nodes
summary: Unlabelled interneurons recorded in stratum oriens may include OLM cells, horizontal bistratified, basket and axo-axonic cells, and various long-range projecting GABAergic cell types. Trilaminar cells (distinct projection neurons) were excluded by their unique high-frequency burst firing. The study compared temporal firing patterns of SOM+ projection cells with locally terminating bistratified and OLM cells.
quotes: The group of unlabeled interneurons recorded in stratum oriens may include O-LM cells, horizontal bistratified, basket and axo-axonic cells and various long-range projecting GABAergic cell types
